August 19, 2024 toolsworkflow

The Tools I Use Every Day

A straightforward list of the tools, apps, and utilities I rely on daily — no affiliate links, no fluff, just the honest stack.

Why bother sharing this?

I always find other people’s tool lists interesting — not because I want to copy their setup, but because the choices reveal something about how they think and work. Here’s mine, as of mid-2024.

Design

Figma is still my primary design tool. I’ve tried the alternatives. They’re not there yet for my workflow. I use it for everything from rough wireframes to polished components.

For quick diagrams and whiteboarding I use Excalidraw. It’s rough on purpose, which means nobody wastes time bikeshedding the visuals in early-stage conversations.

Development

VS Code with a minimal plugin set. I used to have 40 extensions; now I have six. The bloat wasn’t worth the features.

Warp for the terminal. The autocomplete and history search alone make it worth switching from iTerm.

The best tool is the one you stop thinking about. If you’re thinking about your tool, it’s getting in the way.

Writing & notes

Obsidian for personal notes and a second brain. Plain markdown files, stored locally. I’m mildly paranoid about cloud lock-in with things I write, and Obsidian respects that.

For drafting longer pieces (like this one), I switch to iA Writer. The focused environment genuinely helps.

Everything else

  • Arc — browser. Spaces changed how I organise my browser context
  • Linear — project tracking. The best-designed project management tool I’ve used
  • Raycast — launcher. Replaced Alfred and Spotlight completely
  • Cleanshot X — screenshots and screen recording. Indispensable

That’s genuinely it. Every tool is something else to maintain, update, and learn. Fewer, better tools have made me calmer and more focused.
